creator: mah@jump-ing.de
text:
Am 04.09.2007 um 16:24 schrieb Jeremy Messenger:

> If it is what you are expected, then the document needs to be fix.  
> Any users can't just simple 'make PREFIX=/foo install' as what in  
> the handbook has shown.

OK, looks like a lot of people don't want to make it work that way,  
either.

> Also, included fix ports(7). Need to explain more clear something  
> like if you want to install all ports in one prefix, then do the  
> 'make PREFIX=/foo LOCALBASE=/foo install'.

It can be done even simpler: just exchange PREFIX with LOCALBASE:

   make LOCALBASE=/foo install

Works like a charme, as the default of PREFIX is LOCALBASE.

If PREFIX gets pushed out of the handbook, not many people will  
recognise it's non-working dependency building.

I'm looking forward to make patches as soon as the somewhat heated  
discussion has settled.

> I personal still think that the 'make PREFIX=/foo' shouldn't affect  
> on dependencies at all, but I have been using the workarounds for  
> so long time. It is already in my habit.

Thanks.


Markus
